cards.text:
Prabhat's Competitive Nature
Prabhat always had a strong desire to win. He struggled with accepting defeat and felt losing was unbearable.

Perception of Losing
For Prabhat, losing was seen as a failure and something to be avoided at all costs.

Choosing Assured Wins
Prabhat would only engage in games where he was certain he could win. If he anticipated a loss, he avoided playing.

Badminton Expertise
Prabhat excelled at badminton and would play relentlessly in games he mastered.

Surya's Introduction
A new student, Surya, joined Prabhat's school and was recognized for his exceptional badminton skills.

Monthly Match Importance
The last Friday of each month held significance for Prabhat as the coach organized friendly matches during this time.

Match Preparation
Prabhat approached his match with Surya with immense focus, while Surya remained relaxed and jovial.

Cheating Incident
During their badminton match, Prabhat changed the scoreboard while Surya was distracted, ensuring his victory through deceit.

Surya's Reaction
Surya was unaffected by the loss and suggested a rematch, indicating his enjoyment of the game itself.

Prabhat's Restlessness
Despite winning, Prabhat felt unsettled that night, pondering over Surya's carefree attitude.

Surya's Sportsmanship
Surya's effortless enjoyment in participating in various games, regardless of his performance, impressed Prabhat.

Lesson in Enjoyment
Observing Surya, Prabhat learned that true enjoyment in games comes from participation, not just winning.

Transformation in Perspective
Over time, Prabhat's attitude toward games shifted, and he began to appreciate the joy of playing itself.

Newfound Enjoyment
Prabhat started participating in games like hide and seek and found satisfaction even when the games ended.

Sporting Spirit Recognition
Peers recognized Prabhat for his evolving sporting spirit, noting the pleasure of playing with him.
